name: Savory Fried Rice
recipeIngredient: 2 Leeks (small), trimmed and cut into half-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Place rice, cloves, black peppers, turmeric, salt, cardamom, and water in a 2-quart pan.
2. Cover and bring to a boil over high heat.
3. Reduce heat to low and simmer for about 20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed. Remove cloves and black peppers (or warn guests to watch out for them while eating).
4. While the rice is cooking, melt margarine in a wide frying pan. Add leeks and shredded carrot and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
5. Add peas, cashews, and raisins and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
6. When the rice is cooked, stir in the leek mixture.
7. Turn onto a serving platter and garnish with slices of hard-cooked egg and parsley if desired.
Per serving (excluding eggs): 9 g protein, 49 g carbohydrate, 138 mg cholesterol, 321 calories.
Notes: I leave out the eggs and steam the leeks and carrots in the microwave, thus cutting out most of the fat and all of the cholesterol.
